name: Vegetable Casserole with Cornbread Dressing
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons butter, soy margarine, or Canola oil
1 medium sweet potato, chopped into 1-inch cubes
1 cup green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 cup broccoli or cauliflower florets, chopped into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
-------------
CORNBREAD TOPPING
1. In a small bowl, combine 1 egg or 1 Ener-G egg replacer with 1 tablespoon sugar.
Whisk until smooth and creamy.
2. In another small bowl, mix 1/2 cup buttermilk or soymilk with 1/2 cup cornmeal.
3. In a third bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup unbleached white flour (or whole wheat pastry flour),
1 teaspoon baking powder, and 1/2 teaspoon baking soda.
4. Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients and stir until smooth.
5. Stir in 1/2 cup thawed frozen corn, 1 seeded jalapeno, and 1 tablespoon drained pimiento.
6. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
7. In a large skillet, heat oil or margarine over medium heat.
8. Add onions and garlic; sauté until onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
9. Stir in tomatoes and water, then bring to a boil.
10. Add the potato; cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
11. Add sweet potato, green beans, broccoli or cauliflower, and cook, covered, for an additional 5 minutes.
12. Season with salt and pepper. (The vegetables are not fully cooked at this stage.)
13. Stir in peas.
14. Remove from heat and keep covered until topping is ready.
15. Spoon the batter over the vegetables in a lightly oiled casserole dish or pan.
16. Sprinkle pimiento on top and gently press into the batter.
17. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
18. Best served warm, but also good at room temperature.
19. Score lightly with a knife to indicate where to cut for each serving.
Makes 4 to 6 servings. Per serving: 286 calories; 10 grams protein; 5 grams fat; 53 grams carbohydrates;
62 milligrams cholesterol (if buttermilk, eggs, and butter are used); 0 milligrams cholesterol
(if soy margarine, egg replacer, and soymilk are used); 545 milligrams sodium; 9 grams fiber.
